contextBlurb: Newfoundland and Labrador's housing policy is centered on the Newfoundland and Labrador Housing Corporation (NLHC), which is addressing a waitlist that grew 71 per cent between 2021 and 2025. The provincial government has introduced special debate on housing, issued RFPs for transitional and supportive living programs, and is projecting a 20 per cent increase in housing starts for 2025. Rental assistance of up to $1,500 per household per month is available to eligible individuals.

legislation.title: Federal-Provincial Housing and Homelessness Initiative
legislation.summary: A joint federal-provincial housing project administered through the NLHC to address housing and homelessness challenges in Newfoundland and Labrador.

legislation.title: Home Modification Program
legislation.summary: Provides funding to assist homeowners with low-to-moderate incomes to modify their homes, administered through the Newfoundland and Labrador Housing Corporation.

legislation.title: Rental Housing Program for Low-Income Households
legislation.summary: A provincial program providing subsidized rental housing options for low-income individuals and families in Newfoundland and Labrador.

legislation.title: Rental Accommodation Assistance Program
legislation.summary: Provides up to $1,500 per household per month to assist eligible individuals with the cost of rental accommodations, with receipts required to confirm rental costs.

legislation.title: Special Debate on Housing
legislation.summary: The provincial government introduced a special legislative debate on housing, focusing on addressing the social housing waitlist that grew 71 per cent between 2021 and 2025 and increasing housing supply.

legislation.title: Request for Proposals: Transitional and Supportive Living Program in St. John's
legislation.summary: A formal RFP issued by the NLHC to create a new Transitional and Supportive Living Program in St. John's, aimed at helping individuals experiencing homelessness achieve housing stability.
